page: Amazon Web Services c8i.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a massive 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.96xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores. Its memory subsystem delivers exceptional throughput, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 30590.21 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 507.23 GB/sec. The instance also excels in specialized tasks, scoring in the top 10% for software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations. However, while prompt processing for large language models is highly efficient, text generation speeds for smaller models represent a performance tradeoff, falling into the lower percentiles.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.96xlarge offers high compute density and exceptional memory bandwidth, making it highly effective for parallel processing workloads without the overhead of unneeded local storage. It is ideally suited for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, delivering 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic execution, though text generation for small LLMs is a notable tradeoff. With a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-dense, network-reliant workloads. It is best suited for high-performance computing, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86 platform featuring 384 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding parallel processing workloads.
